summary: Agent infrastructure signals: sandbox escapes, memory persistence, MCP tooling, and evaluation frameworks dominate. Key themes include local-first memory, secure sandboxing, and multi-agent orchestration.

candidates.title: ruvnet/ruflo - Agent meta-harness
candidates.why_it_matters: Leading agent meta-harness with adaptive memory and multi-agent swarms

candidates.title: TencentCloud/CubeSandbox - Secure sandbox
candidates.why_it_matters: Instant, concurrent, lightweight sandbox for AI agents

candidates.title: MAMA - Local-first operating memory
candidates.why_it_matters: Local-first memory with SQLite and on-device embeddings

candidates.title: openagent - Personal AI assistant
candidates.why_it_matters: Next-gen assistant with computer-use, browser-use, and coding agent

candidates.title: mcp-ai-memory - Semantic memory MCP server
candidates.why_it_matters: Production-ready MCP server for semantic memory management

candidates.title: harness-code - Multi-model coding agent
candidates.why_it_matters: Desktop coding agent supporting 340+ models via OpenRouter

candidates.title: agloom - Production agent framework
candidates.why_it_matters: Nine execution patterns, persistent memory, MCP, and observability

candidates.title: rusty-imap-mcp - Security-first IMAP MCP
candidates.why_it_matters: Security-first MCP server for agentic email access

candidates.title: nevoflux-agent - Browser automation agent
candidates.why_it_matters: Rust-based browser/desktop automation with MCP and WASM skills

candidates.title: calibrate - AI agent evaluation framework
candidates.why_it_matters: Framework for evaluating speech, LLM, and end-to-end agent simulations

candidates.title: StackUnderflow - Local agent observability
candidates.why_it_matters: Local observability for coding agents with offline replay

candidates.title: substrate - Multi-agent orchestration daemon
candidates.why_it_matters: Daemon for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents

gaps: No strong signals for deployment or storage infrastructure
Limited coverage of agent security beyond sandbox escapes
